Optional feature weights rescale marker columns computing cost. Optional diagonal boost, anchor cost bonuses, label-guided penalties modify normalised cost. entropic term ε⋅KL(P∥𝟏)\varepsilon \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P \,\|\, \mathbf{1}) yields Gibbs kernel Kij=exp⁡(−Cij/ε)K_{ij} = \exp(-C_{ij}/\varepsilon) used primal-domain solver. log-domain annealing solvers use equivalent log-potential updates avoid explicitly materialising underflowing kernel. title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: new batch carries batch effect differs marker, realistic reagent lot change inter-instrument drift channel shifts amount. small per-cell jitter reflects run--run variation, anchor displacements span low-rank subspace rather one identical vector (matters subspace correction ).
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(42) p <- 5L  # Old batch: two well-separated populations old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean = -2, sd = 0.6), ncol = p),   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean =  2, sd = 0.6), ncol = p) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("CD", seq_len(p))  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(4, 4, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 50 ) reference #> <somalign_reference> #>   features: 5 #>   reference nodes: 16 #>   labelled nodes: 0 batch_shift <- c(0.6, -0.4, 0.5, 0.0, 0.3)          # per-marker technical offset new_data <- old_data +   matrix(batch_shift, nrow = nrow(old_data), ncol = p, byrow = TRUE) +   matrix(rnorm(nrow(old_data) * p, sd = 0.15), ncol = p)   # per-cell jitter  query <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(4, 4, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 50 ) query #> <somalign_query> #>   samples: 120 #>   features: 5 #>   query nodes: 16

title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: rho_anchor controls much anchor bonus reduces normalized OT cost. rho_anchor = 0 bonus vanishes result equals somalign_fit(). rho_anchor grows, anchor-supported pairs get progressively cheaper. bonus exceeds normalized cost pair, pair’s effective cost clamps zero; plan spreads mass among zero-cost pairs entropic regularization rather differential anchor support, large values stop concentrating mass . practical upper bound rho_anchor * max_A / n_anchors <= 1, max_A highest anchor count single node pair. text: default correction = "cost_bonus" applies OT-derived shift across markers. reagent- calibration-driven batch effects correct: displacement genuinely technical removed. assumption breaks new batch contains populations activation states absent reference. full-space shift pushes cells toward reference coordinates erases distinguishing biology. anchor pair links biological unit measured batches, row vector anchor_old - anchor_new observes batch displacement biological contribution. SVD n_anchors p displacement matrix isolates batch directions; call V_batch. Restricting node shifts V_batch corrects batch component leaves biology orthogonal untouched. batch_subspace element carries V (p rank), rank, variance_explained. default variance_threshold = 0.9 selects smallest rank r whose top r squared singular values account least 90% total. pure uniform offset collapses rank 1; heterogeneous batch effect, like per-marker shift plus jitter , occupies dimensions. Raising threshold 1.0 keeps every direction, rank grows without changing anchor data: correction = "" applies anchor cost bonus OT solve restricts resulting shifts V_batch: rho_anchor effect correction = "subspace", since cost bonus apply; "" tuning guidance "cost_bonus" holds. simple uniform offset three modes give similar corrected positions. difference matters anchor coverage uneven query carries populations reference represent.

what: Corrected marker expression for downstream analysis
title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: Label transfer primary product, downstream steps, UMAP differential-expression contrast, want corrected expression matrix rather per-cell label. somalign_correct_expression() returns one: cells--markers matrix, smoothed across cell’s nearest SOM nodes. anchored fit correction = "subspace" "" (like fit_sub ), correction confined anchor-estimated batch subspace, variation orthogonal batch direction stays intact. two-pass fit carries anchor subspace; somalign_correct_expression() applies full two-pass shift instead. Either kind fit works; plain somalign_fit() carries batch correction rejected. Treat output aid visualisation differential expression, primary product. correction preserves orthogonal structure, within batch subspace reduces rather fully removes distance populations. Run somalign_topology_audit(fit_sub) first confirm correction warranted, populations sitting close together along batch direction can drawn toward one another. compare cell-type composition abundance across batches, prefer direct projection columns somalign_results() compositional (centred log-ratio) transform.

what: Choosing a mode, and when to skip anchors
title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: somalign_fit_anchored() fits set samples (QC pools, reference standards, proficiency panels) ran batches, giving ground-truth node-pair correspondences. Anchor pairs need cover full codebook; even sparse coverage constrains plan covered pairs, uncovered nodes fall back standard objective. remeasured samples exist, use somalign_fit() directly. Fabricated imputed anchor pairs add information misguide transport. query-biology must preserved, use correction = "subspace" ""; full-space shift appropriate (batch dominates signal-preservation needed), default "cost_bonus" enough. See vignette("two-pass", package = "somalign") two-pass decomposition, handles large global offsets without remeasured anchors.

code: Y_old   <- model.matrix(~ labels_old - 1) colnames(Y_old) <- levels(factor(labels_old)) old_scaled <- scale(old_data, center = ref$center, scale = ref$scale)  ss_ref <- kohonen::supersom(   list(old_scaled, Y_old),   grid      = grid_small,   rlen      = 20,   keep.data = TRUE ) ref_ss <- somalign_reference_from_som(   ss_ref,   center         = ref$center,   scale          = ref$scale,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled",   labels         = "codebook" ) stopifnot(inherits(ref_ss, "somalign_reference")) stopifnot(!is.null(ref_ss$label_prob)) cat("Nodes:", nrow(ref_ss$codebook), "  label_prob rows:", nrow(ref_ss$label_prob), "\n") #> Nodes: 9   label_prob rows: 9

title: API smoke test: all public functions
text: Anchor-regularized alignment using remeasured QC samples. rho_anchor = 0 short-circuits plain-OT path emits message: correction = "subspace" estimates batch directions anchor displacement SVD restricts node shifts subspace, preserving orthogonal biology:
code:
anc_idx    <- c(1:10, 31:40) anchor_old <- old_data[anc_idx, ] anchor_new <- new_data[anc_idx, ]  fit_anc <- somalign_fit_anchored(   qry, ref,   anchor_old = anchor_old,   anchor_new = anchor_new,   rho_anchor = 1.0 ) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.14); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. stopifnot(inherits(fit_anc, "somalign_anchored_fit")) stopifnot(inherits(fit_anc, "somalign_fit")) cat("n_anchors:        ", fit_anc$anchors$n_anchors, "
") #> n_anchors:         20 cat("coverage_fraction:", round(fit_anc$anchors$coverage_fraction, 3), "
") #> coverage_fraction: 0.667 fit_anc0 <- somalign_fit_anchored(   qry, ref,   anchor_old = anchor_old,   anchor_new = anchor_new,   rho_anchor = 0 ) #> `rho_anchor = 0`: the anchor cost bonus is inactive. Use `somalign_fit()` for equivalent results. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.13); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. stopifnot(inherits(fit_anc0, "somalign_anchored_fit")) stopifnot(fit_anc0$anchors$rho_anchor == 0) fit_sub <- somalign_fit_anchored(   qry, ref,   anchor_old = anchor_old,   anchor_new = anchor_new,   correction = "subspace" ) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.13); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. stopifnot(inherits(fit_sub, "somalign_anchored_fit")) stopifnot(!is.null(fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace)) cat("Correction mode:    ", fit_sub$anchors$correction, "
") #> Correction mode:     subspace cat("Batch subspace rank:", fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace$rank, "
") #> Batch subspace rank: 1 cat("Variance explained: ",     round(fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace$variance_explained, 3), "
") #> Variance explained:  1

title: API smoke test: all public functions
text: Two-pass alignment: first OT pass epsilon_global estimates mass-weighted global shift, subtracts query codebook, second pass epsilon_local captures residual per-node offsets. Exposes $two_pass addition standard somalign_fit interface. somalign_results() somalign_diagnostics() accept two-pass fit unchanged:
code:
fit2 <- somalign_fit_two_pass(qry, ref, epsilon_global = 0.3, epsilon_local = 0.1) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.49); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.14); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. stopifnot(inherits(fit2, "somalign_fit")) stopifnot(!is.null(fit2$two_pass)) stopifnot(all(c("global_shift", "global_shift_norm",                 "epsilon_global", "epsilon_local",                 "batch_subspace") %in% names(fit2$two_pass))) cat("Global shift norm:  ", round(fit2$two_pass$global_shift_norm, 4), "
") #> Global shift norm:   0.2949 cat("Batch subspace rank:", fit2$two_pass$batch_subspace$rank, "
") #> Batch subspace rank: 2 res2  <- somalign_results(fit2) diag2 <- somalign_diagnostics(fit2) stopifnot(is.data.frame(res2)) stopifnot(all(c("solver", "ot", "nodes", "projection") %in% names(diag2))) cat("Converged:", diag2$solver$converged, "
") #> Converged: TRUE

text: eight mean = 4 samples population reference never saw. land far every reference node, final_status marks outside_reference label transferred read provisional. real samples project inside confident labels, though batch-shifted ones also cross distance threshold, exactly final_status surface.
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(2) old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(30 * 6, mean = -1), ncol = 6),   matrix(rnorm(30 * 6, mean = 1), ncol = 6) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("marker_", seq_len(ncol(old_data))) old_labels <- rep(c("old_low", "old_high"), each = 30)  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   labels = old_labels,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 10 )  new_data <- rbind(   old_data[1:15, ] + 0.15,   old_data[46:60, ] + 0.25,   matrix(rnorm(8 * 6, mean = 4), ncol = 6) ) colnames(new_data) <- colnames(old_data)  new_scaled_for_som <- sweep(   sweep(new_data[, reference$features], 2, reference$center, "-"),   2,   reference$scale,   "/" )  new_som <- kohonen::som(   new_scaled_for_som,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 10 )  query <- somalign_query(   new_data,   reference,   som_query = new_som,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled" )  fit <- somalign_fit(query, reference) #> somalign_fit: 3 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.26); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. sample_metadata <- data.frame(batch = rep("new_batch", nrow(new_data))) results <- somalign_results(fit, data = sample_metadata)  # A focused view: the transferred label alongside the direct-projection status. # The Quick start vignette documents the full column set. head(results[, c(   "sample_id",   "old_som_label",   "final_status",   "outside_reference_distance",   "transferred_label",   "transferred_label_confidence",   "transferred_label_accepted" )]) #>   sample_id old_som_label     final_status outside_reference_distance #> 1         1       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #> 2         2       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #> 3         3       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #> 4         4       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #> 5         5       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #> 6         6       old_low inside_reference                      FALSE #>   transferred_label transferred_label_confidence transferred_label_accepted #> 1           old_low                    0.9993081                       TRUE #> 2          old_high                    0.6031686                       TRUE #> 3              <NA>                           NA                      FALSE #> 4           old_low                    0.9993081                       TRUE #> 5           old_low                    0.9993081                       TRUE #> 6           old_low                    0.9993081                       TRUE

title: Quick start
text: Aligning query reference takes three calls: build reference, prepare query, fit. example uses small simulated dataset two populations, low high. query two populations remeasured second batch offsets population differently, kind shift single global recentring undo node-level alignment can.
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(1)  # Reference: two labelled populations old <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(80, mean = -1), ncol = 4),   matrix(rnorm(80, mean =  1), ncol = 4) ) colnames(old) <- paste0("f", seq_len(ncol(old))) labels <- rep(c("low", "high"), each = 20)  # Query: same populations, second batch, each population offset differently query <- old query[labels == "low", ]  <- query[labels == "low", ]  + 0.8 query[labels == "high", ] <- query[labels == "high", ] - 0.6  # 1. Train the reference SOM (labels attach the classes to transfer) reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old,   labels = labels,   grid   = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen   = 5 )  # 2. Prepare the query (scaled with the reference centre and scale) query_obj <- somalign_query(   query,   reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 5 )  # 3. Fit: align the query and reference SOMs, transfer labels fit <- somalign_fit(query_obj, reference) #> somalign_fit: 1 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.01); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details.

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: Reach somalign_fit_two_pass() batch effect large global component: reagent lot changes, instrument calibration drift, environmental factors shift every population roughly direction, layered top smaller population-specific displacements. single OT pass struggles shape. small epsilon captures fine structure destabilises global offset dwarfs typical inter-node distance; large epsilon smooths away instability loses population-level resolution. somalign_fit_two_pass() splits problem two. first pass larger epsilon_global estimates mass-weighted mean node displacement, global shift, subtracts query codebook. second pass finer epsilon_local captures residual population-specific offsets. final per-node correction sum passes, total displacement per sample matches direct single-pass alignment epsilon_local. Subtracting global component first makes second-pass problem better conditioned. two-pass procedure refines correction (per-node shift field), somalign treats diagnostic rather product. corrected coordinates can -merge distinct populations (see somalign_topology_audit()) batch-corrected expression matrix. primary deliverable remains label transfer.

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: new batch carries dominant global offset shared populations, plus smaller population-specific residual second pass can capture. two residuals equal opposite across balanced populations, cell mean zero. keeps residual biasing estimated global shift, stays aligned shared offset even though mass-weighted mean SOM nodes rather plain cell average.
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(42) p <- 4L  old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean = -2, sd = 0.5), ncol = p),   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean =  2, sd = 0.5), ncol = p) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("CD", seq_len(p)) old_labels <- rep(c("pop_A", "pop_B"), each = 60L)  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   labels = old_labels,   grid   = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen   = 30 ) reference #> <somalign_reference> #>   features: 4 #>   reference nodes: 9 #>   labelled nodes: 7 global_shift <- c(0.5, 0.4, 0.45, 0.35)          # shared technical offset pop_delta    <- c(0.15, -0.12, 0.10, -0.08)      # population-specific residual  pop_shift <- rbind(   matrix( pop_delta, 60L, p, byrow = TRUE),      # pop_A: +delta   matrix(-pop_delta, 60L, p, byrow = TRUE)       # pop_B: -delta )  set.seed(43) local_noise <- matrix(rnorm(nrow(old_data) * p, 0, 0.08), ncol = p) new_data    <- old_data +   matrix(global_shift, nrow(old_data), p, byrow = TRUE) +   pop_shift + local_noise  query <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 30 ) query #> <somalign_query> #>   samples: 120 #>   features: 4 #>   query nodes: 9

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: $two_pass slot records estimated global shift epsilon values: global_shift points correction direction, query codebook toward reference, sign opposite true batch offset. node shifts mass-weighted mean first-pass OT transport vectors, magnitude reference-scaled units, raw units, equal raw feature offset directly. matters practice direction: feature’s sign oppose known batch offset, features larger displacements show larger absolute values. norm global_shift_norm gives one scalar measure global displacement, comparable across batches experiments.
code:
fit2 <- somalign_fit_two_pass(   query, reference,   epsilon_global = 0.3,   epsilon_local  = 0.1 ) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.61); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.16); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. fit2 #> <somalign_fit> #>   label transfer: 100.0% of cells accepted across 2 class(es); median confidence 1.00, median margin 1.00 #>   solver: internal  |  query nodes: 9  |  reference nodes: 9  |  transport mass: 1.114 cat("Estimated global shift (correction direction, query -> reference):
") #> Estimated global shift (correction direction, query -> reference): print(round(fit2$two_pass$global_shift, 3)) #>    CD1    CD2    CD3    CD4  #> -0.238 -0.184 -0.198 -0.145 cat("
True batch offset (positive = new batch above old):
") #>  #> True batch offset (positive = new batch above old): print(round(global_shift, 3)) #> [1] 0.50 0.40 0.45 0.35 cat("
Global shift magnitude:", round(fit2$two_pass$global_shift_norm, 3), "
") #>  #> Global shift magnitude: 0.388

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: $two_pass$batch_subspace element summarises principal directions first-pass correction field: read-diagnostic. Rank 1 means pass-1 node shifts nearly collinear, expected single global offset dominates. Higher rank means first-pass correction heterogeneous directions across codebook, points toward spatially varied batch effect like per-population residual . subspace used correction: total shift residual pass-2 shift plus global shift g. first-pass OT sees original unmodified codebook rather controlled anchors, diagnostic can conflate batch effects biology. Read descriptively, geometric estimate true batch direction. anchor-based analogue serve geometric estimate, see vignette("anchor-samples", package = "somalign"). variance_threshold argument controls rank selection diagnostic :
code:
bs <- fit2$two_pass$batch_subspace cat("Batch subspace rank (pass 1):            ", bs$rank, "
") #> Batch subspace rank (pass 1):             3 cat("Variance explained by leading directions:", round(bs$variance_explained, 3), "
") #> Variance explained by leading directions: 0.957 fit2b <- somalign_fit_two_pass(query, reference, variance_threshold = 1.0) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.61); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.16); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. cat("Rank at variance_threshold = 1.0:", fit2b$two_pass$batch_subspace$rank, "
") #> Rank at variance_threshold = 1.0: 4  # Correction is unchanged regardless of variance_threshold cat("Node shifts identical:", isTRUE(all.equal(fit2$node_shifts, fit2b$node_shifts)), "
") #> Node shifts identical: TRUE

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: somalign_fit() somalign_fit_two_pass() accept label_guided = TRUE, applies large cost penalty node pairs whose dominant labels disagree. reference labels known batch scramble population boundaries, penalty concentrates transport onto -label pairs reduces spurious cross-cluster mass. label_guided = TRUE requires query$label_prob non-NULL. standard somalign_query() workflow kohonen::som() leaves field NULL; populated kohonen::supersom() kohonen::xyf() SOM label code layer passed som_query. Build query SOM one-hot label layer, supply via som_query, set label_guided = TRUE: Label-guided alignment works way somalign_fit(). labels noisy, batch shifts populations across label boundaries, penalty can destabilise OT plan. Check somalign_diagnostics() compare somalign_sensitivity_grid() using label-transferred assignments downstream.
code: # One-hot label matrix for new_data (requires knowing new-batch labels) Y <- model.matrix(~ old_labels - 1) colnames(Y) <- levels(factor(old_labels))  # Train a supersom with the label layer new_scaled <- scale(new_data, center = reference$center, scale = reference$scale) query_ss   <- kohonen::supersom(   list(new_scaled, Y),   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 30,   keep.data = TRUE )  query_labeled <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   som_query      = query_ss,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled" )  fit_lg <- somalign_fit_two_pass(   query_labeled, reference,   label_guided = TRUE )

what: Choosing between two-pass and the alternatives
title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: Two-pass alignment helps global batch offset large relative epsilon_local: large enough single low-epsilon pass struggles route mass across full displacement, single high-epsilon pass loses population-level resolution. explicit global subtraction removes tension. batch shift small OT problem already well-conditioned single epsilon, somalign_fit() simpler equally effective. remeasured control samples available, somalign_fit_anchored() correction = "subspace" offers geometrically grounded, signal-preserving correction alternative two-pass decomposition. See vignette("anchor-samples", package = "somalign").

what: Scoring labels against a known truth
title: Validating and tuning label transfer
text: somalign_label_metrics() scores predicted labels ground truth. reports overall accuracy, macro-averaged F1, multiclass Matthews correlation coefficient (Gorodkin’s ), per-class precision, recall F1, confusion matrix. Metrics computed accepted predictions; coverage records fraction cells . accepted argument marks predictions score; cells accepted count abstentions reported coverage accuracy_all rather errors.
code: library(somalign)  truth <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell", "Mono"), each = 20) pred  <- truth pred[c(1, 25, 45)] <- c("Bcell", "Tcell", "Tcell")   # three errors of 60  m <- somalign_label_metrics(pred, truth) m #> <somalign_label_metrics> #>   accuracy = 0.9500  macro_f1 = 0.9504  MCC = 0.9254 #>   scored = 60  coverage = 100.0%  accuracy_all = 0.9500 m$per_class #>   class precision recall        f1 support #> 1 Bcell 0.9500000   0.95 0.9500000      20 #> 2  Mono 1.0000000   0.95 0.9743590      20 #> 3 Tcell 0.9047619   0.95 0.9268293      20

what: Tuning the transport plan for accuracy
title: Validating and tuning label transfer
text: somalign_select_epsilon() picks regularisation strength plan geometry alone, without reference labels. labelled data available, somalign_tune() selects transport parameters cross-validated label accuracy instead. sweeps parameter grid, runs held-cross-validation point, reports every metric operating point can chosen deliberately. default objective multiclass MCC. Read alongside coverage: MCC accuracy scored accepted cells , large epsilon can raise abstaining hard cells. macro_f1 falls rare classes abstained away, makes coverage-robust target imbalanced data.
code: tuned <- somalign_tune(   x, lab, grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   param_grid = data.frame(epsilon = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2)),   k = 3, rlen = 20, metric = "mcc" ) tuned$grid #>   epsilon rho_query rho_ref diagonal_boost feature_weights accuracy macro_f1 #> 1    0.05         1       1              0            none        1        1 #> 2    0.10         1       1              0            none        1        1 #> 3    0.20         1       1              0            none        1        1 #>   mcc coverage          ece #> 1   1        1 0.000000e+00 #> 2   1        1 0.000000e+00 #> 3   1        1 4.850388e-10

title: Validating and tuning label transfer
text: Anchors quality-control samples run batches. somalign_fit_anchored() bias transport plan toward anchor-supported node pairs. somalign_anchor_benefit() quantifies much improve label transfer sweeping anchor strength rho_anchor known query label; rho_anchor = 0 -anchor baseline. Anchors help batch effect population-specific severe enough plain plan mis-maps lineages, rho_anchor well 1. mild global batch shifts plain plan already succeeds, anchor lift near zero.
code: set.seed(1) shift <- 2 ref_x <- rbind(matrix(rnorm(120 * 3, -3, 0.5), ncol = 3),                matrix(rnorm(120 * 3,  3, 0.5), ncol = 3)) colnames(ref_x) <- paste0("m", seq_len(3)) ref_lab <- rep(c("low", "high"), each = 120) ref <- somalign_train_reference(ref_x, labels = ref_lab,                                 grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"), rlen = 20)  qry_x <- ref_x + shift qry <- somalign_query(qry_x, ref, grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"), rlen = 20)  anc_idx <- c(1:30, 121:150) ab <- somalign_anchor_benefit(   qry, ref, ref_lab,   anchor_old = ref_x[anc_idx, ],   anchor_new = ref_x[anc_idx, ] + shift,   rho_grid = c(0, 1, 10, 100) ) ab$grid #>   rho_anchor accuracy macro_f1 mcc coverage          ece #> 1          0        1        1   1        1 1.432153e-04 #> 2          1        1        1   1        1 6.966324e-05 #> 3         10        1        1   1        1 2.906399e-06 #> 4        100        1        1   1        1 2.164835e-06

title: Validating and tuning label transfer
text: Label transfer gives cell cluster; comparing cluster abundance across batches separate, compositional problem. Cluster frequencies sum one, correlating raw proportions dominated largest clusters understates well rarer clusters recovered. Quantify abundance centred log-ratio (CLR) transform per-sample cluster counts instead. crumblr package computes CLR together count-based precision weights suitable differential-abundance models: Use direct projection (old_som_unit) composition, corrected one; CLR abundance profile reproducible across batches, whereas absolute per-cluster proportions approximate. abundance step specifically, prefer soft projection hard nearest-node counts. Hard assignment sends cell single node counts node’s cluster. cell near cluster boundary therefore contributes whole unit one side, small batch shift can flip side falls , inflating sampling variance per-sample cluster proportions. somalign_soft_frequencies() instead spreads cell nearest reference nodes, giving smoother per-sample frequency profile reproduces better across batches. Pass node--cluster map node_groups grouping interest coarser reference labels: -likely label unchanged softening; abundance estimate smoothed.
code: library(crumblr) counts <- table(sample_id, transferred_label)   # samples x clusters, integer cobj <- crumblr(as.matrix(counts))               # CLR in cobj$E, weights in cobj$weights # node2cluster: length-n_nodes map from reference node to metacluster. # Use normalize = FALSE for crumblr, which models counts, not frequencies. counts <- somalign_soft_frequencies(fit, group = sample_id,                                     node_groups = node2cluster, normalize = FALSE) # crumblr wants a plain integer samples-by-cluster matrix, so drop the # somalign_soft_frequencies class and round the soft counts to integers. counts <- matrix(as.integer(round(counts)), nrow(counts), ncol(counts),                  dimnames = dimnames(counts)) cobj <- crumblr::crumblr(counts)   # CLR in cobj$E, precision weights in cobj$weights

"": applies cost bonus OT solve restricts resulting shifts \(V_{\text{batch}}\). "subspace" "" expose fit$anchors$batch_subspace (list V, rank, variance_explained). "cost_bonus" sets NULL. Higher epsilon blurs transport plan across wider neighbourhood, causing corrected codebook collapse biologically distinct populations (H0 component merging). Subspace-restricted modes ("subspace" "") substantially reduce merging given epsilon shifts confined batch-variation subspace, leaving orthogonal biological variation intact. result, choosing epsilon involves genuine trade-: higher epsilon numerically stable Sinkhorn solver, lower epsilon preserves topology. committing epsilon, run somalign_epsilon_sweep(..., topology = TRUE) alongside somalign_select_epsilon() inspect phase-transition criterion biggest_merge_mass_frac column – two criteria can disagree, especially small epsilon near numerical instability. text: som trained kohonen SOM object (kohonen::som(), kohonen::supersom(), kohonen::xyf()) keep.data = TRUE (kohonen default). center Named numeric vector reference feature centers, one per feature.  Required. scale Named numeric vector reference feature scales, one per feature (must strictly positive).  Required. codebook_space Coordinate system SOM codebook. "reference_scaled" SOM trained data already transformed center scale; "raw" SOM trained raw feature values transformed reference-scaled space use. labels "codebook" (default) reads per-node label distribution Y-layer codes[[2]] enables label transfer.  "none" skips label extraction disables label transfer regardless whether Y-layer present. quantile_probs Quantile levels per-node distance thresholds. Passed somalign_reference_from_nodes. distance_chunk_size Number cells process per chunk computing X-space cell--node distances.  Reduce memory tight; increase faster throughput. text: somalign_reference object, identical structure output somalign_reference built without reprojecting cells.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
text: X codebook codes[[1]] — reference node positions feature space, already used existing API. Node masses tabulate(som$unit.classif) — exact counts training cells, zero cost. Label probabilities codes[[2]] — supervised Y-layer codebook xyf/supersom object.  row per-node distribution cell-type labels.  Absent plain som() objects, case label transfer disabled. Distance quantiles Recomputed reference-scaled X-space embedded som$data[[1]] using cell's known unit.classif assignment.  O(N \(\times\) p) argmax O(N \(\times\) nodes) memory peak. Note partition semantics.  xyf/supersom trained equal layer weights, unit.classif reflects joint X+Y (label-weighted) assignment, pure-X nearest-node assignment.  Node masses therefore match SOM's supervised partition rather somalign's X-geometry.  Distance quantiles still computed X-space outside-reference threshold scale somalign_fit()'s query distances.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
code: set.seed(1) n <- 120 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 2), nrow = n, ncol = 2,             dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) Y <- cbind(A = rep(c(1, 0), each = n / 2),            B = rep(c(0, 1), each = n / 2)) som_obj <- kohonen::supersom(list(X, Y),                              grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),                              rlen = 5, keep.data = TRUE) center <- colMeans(X) scale  <- apply(X, 2, sd) X_scaled <- scale(X, center = center, scale = scale) som_scaled <- kohonen::supersom(list(X_scaled, Y),                                 grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),                                 rlen = 5, keep.data = TRUE) ref <- somalign_reference_from_som(som_scaled,                                    center = center, scale = scale,                                    codebook_space = "reference_scaled")

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
code: somalign_results(   fit,   data = NULL,   outside_pvalue_threshold = NULL,   include_correction = TRUE )

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
text: fit somalign_fit object. data Optional data frame append result columns. outside_pvalue_threshold Optional numeric [0, 1]. supplied, adds boolean outside_reference_pvalue_flag column, TRUE outside_reference_pvalue < outside_pvalue_threshold. NULL (default) omits column. include_correction Logical. FALSE, drops auxiliary correction columns (corrected_som_unit, corrected_som_distance, corrected_som_distance_threshold, corrected_outside_reference_distance, correction_norm) label-transfer-focused result. corrected coordinates diagnostic, batch-corrected expression product; see package's topology audit can -merge populations. Default TRUE (unchanged behaviour).

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
text: data frame direct corrected projection columns, plus transferred_label_second, transferred_label_second_confidence, transferred_label_margin triaging low-margin label transfers, outside_reference_surprisal, outside_reference_pvalue, outside_reference_top_marker: calibrated chi-squared alternative outside_reference_distance weights per-marker deviations query cell's assigned reference node node's per-marker variance (reference$node_var). NA reference built without node_var (e.g. compute_node_var = FALSE, reference built feature). chi-squared calibration assumes diagonal-Gaussian node model; anti-conservative heavy-tailed (e.g. lognormal) marker distributions, remains useful relative ranking anomalous cells regardless.

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
text: transferred_label query node's top label transfer choice, argmax correspondence %*% reference$label_prob. two labels receive close probability mass, argmax can brittle: cell can land node whose true dominant population clean, yet still get runner-label narrowly lost top spot. transferred_label_second transferred_label_margin (top confidence minus second confidence, query node) expose directly, close calls can triaged downstream instead silently trusted face value.

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
text: comparing cell-type composition abundance across batches, use direct projection columns (old_som_unit, old_som_label), corrected ones. barycentric correction can -merge distinct populations (see somalign_topology_audit()) improve, can worsen, cross-batch reproducibility composition. Quantify abundance compositionally rather raw frequencies: apply centred log-ratio (CLR) transform per-sample cluster counts (example crumblr package, also supplies count-based precision weights downstream differential-abundance models). Raw proportions dominated largest clusters understate reproducibility rarer ones; CLR profile reproducible quantity. Absolute per-cluster proportions node-level frequencies remain approximate treated precise.

what: Select epsilon from an epsilon-sweep curve — somalign_select_epsilon
title: Select epsilon from an epsilon-sweep curve — somalign_select_epsilon
text: Sweeps grid epsilon values via somalign_epsilon_sweep() selects recommended value using one three criteria: susceptibility (critical-epsilon) peak, mutual-information-vs-cost elbow, smallest epsilon retaining target fraction maximum mutual information.

title: Select epsilon from an epsilon-sweep curve — somalign_select_epsilon
code: somalign_select_epsilon(   query,   reference,   epsilon = c(0.02, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1),   rho_query = 1,   rho_ref = 1,   method = c("critical", "elbow", "entropy_fraction"),   entropy_fraction = 0.9,   solver = c("log_domain", "internal", "auto", "annealing"),   max_iter = 1000,   tol = 1e-07,   diagonal_boost = 0,   label_guided = FALSE,   parallel = FALSE,   anneal_start = 10,   anneal_factor = NULL,   anneal_stages = 10L )

title: Select epsilon from an epsilon-sweep curve — somalign_select_epsilon
text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. epsilon Numeric vector candidate epsilon values, NULL use default grid. Default c(0.02, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0). rho_query, rho_ref Marginal relaxations passed OT solver. method Character. "critical" (default) uses susceptibility peak's recommended epsilon (0.3 * epsilon_c). "elbow" uses max-second-difference rule mutual-information-vs-cost curve. "entropy_fraction" chooses largest (-regularized) epsilon still retains entropy_fraction * max(mutual_information). entropy_fraction Numeric (0, 1]. Target fraction maximum mutual information method = "entropy_fraction". title: Soft (probabilistic) label projection for query cells — somalign_soft_labels
text: Hard projection assigns cell single nearest node inherits node's label, discarding cell's position within node's Voronoi region. label boundary makes assignment discontinuous 0/1 decision, small batch shift can flip cell's label boundary cell contributes full unit one label hedging. Soft projection instead spreads cell nearest nodes' labels, removes boundary discontinuity reduces quantisation variance downstream per-sample frequency estimates. changes frequency estimate, -likely label: max.col() soft-label matrix typically matches hard label.

title: Soft (probabilistic) label projection for query cells — somalign_soft_labels
text: Soft projection applies acceptance --reference gating. Unlike hard path somalign_results() (flags cells via outside_reference_distance / transferred_label_accepted can suppress low-confidence transfers), every cell contributes soft distribution according distance reference nodes alone. Cells fall outside reference still projected onto nearest nodes. need exclude --reference cells, filter somalign_results() aggregating, subset query.

code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("kohonen", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(1)   x <- rbind(matrix(rnorm(200, -2), ncol = 2), matrix(rnorm(200, 2), ncol = 2))   colnames(x) <- c("f1", "f2")   lab <- rep(c("low", "high"), each = 100)   tuned <- somalign_tune(x, lab, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal"),     param_grid = data.frame(epsilon = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2)), k = 3)   tuned$best_params$epsilon } #> [1] 0.05 # }

title: somalign 0.99.5
text: somalign_reference_from_som(codebook_space = "raw") now reorders SOM’s training data codebook feature order scaling, . previous order applied feature’s centre/scale positionally matrix yet aligned, silently corrupting node distances, distance quantiles, node_var whenever som$data[[1]] columns ordered differently codebook. somalign_fit_two_pass() now zeroes shift correction_allowed == FALSE nodes, matching invariant single-pass Laplacian smoother already enforces (somalign_correct_expression()). Previously nodes still received global batch shift g stored corrected projection despite flagged uncorrected. Subspace sensitivity: .somalign_anchor_leverage() now zero-pads leave-one-subspace full-data rank comparing principal angles, fixing wrong (warning-triggering) leverage values leaving anchor changed variance-threshold-selected rank. somalign_query_from_som() now stores label_prob, matching somalign_query() documented identical structure. somalign_calibration() reports n_total coverage alongside n, documentation now states ECE/MCE/Brier computed scored (non-abstained) predictions — calibration can compared fairly across methods abstain different rates. log-domain / annealing solver’s log_Z now uses symmetric entropic dual objective <,f> + <b,g> - epsilon * sum(P) instead row-marginal-quantity omitted column-marginal contribution. Input codebooks now validated finite, label-guidance penalty computed finite-safe base.

title: somalign 0.99.5
text: somalign_soft_labels() somalign_soft_frequencies() add soft (probabilistic) projection: query cell projected onto k nearest reference nodes Gaussian kernel given distribution labels (node-level grouping via node_groups, e.g. node--metacluster map), rather single hard nearest-node label. Aggregated per sample, reduces quantisation/boundary variance hard cluster proportions improves cross-batch reproducibility cluster-abundance profiles (validated: 20 pilot-vs-BMV repeat samples improved 0.932 0.957 median metacluster CLR weighted-r, robust across k). -likely label unchanged; frequency estimate smoothed. Reuses k-NN kernel machinery somalign_correct_expression(). somalign_correct_expression() returns cell-level (cells markers) batch-corrected marker expression matrix downstream visualisation differential expression. correction confined anchor-estimated batch subspace smoothed across cell’s nearest SOM nodes Gaussian kernel, preserving variation orthogonal batch direction. Requires subspace-aware fit somalign_fit_anchored(correction = "subspace"/"") somalign_fit_two_pass(). auxiliary correction aid, primary label-transfer product; see ?somalign_correct_expression vignette("anchor-samples"). somalign_reference(), somalign_train_reference(), somalign_reference_from_som() now compute store reference$node_var (per-node per-marker variance reference-scaled space) default (compute_node_var = TRUE; set FALSE opt ). somalign_reference_from_nodes() accepts pre-computed node_var matrix directly. somalign_results() uses add calibrated chi-squared alternative distance-quantile outside_reference_distance flag: outside_reference_surprisal, outside_reference_pvalue, outside_reference_top_marker (single worst-contributing marker per cell – useful pinpointing marker-specific batch artifacts). optional outside_pvalue_threshold argument adds boolean outside_reference_pvalue_flag column. NA reference lacks node_var. somalign_results() now exposes transferred_label_second, transferred_label_second_confidence, transferred_label_margin (top confidence minus second confidence, query node’s label transfer). transferred_label argmax label transport probabilities can brittle two labels receive close mass; margin lets callers triage low-confidence label transfers (e.g. cells nodes top runner-label nearly tied) without re-deriving fit$label_transfer. somalign_query_from_som() — zero-reprojection query constructor reuses som$unit.classif directly per-cell node assignments, bypassing O(N × nodes) nearest-code search somalign_query() performs. Accepts optional pre-transformed codebook (e.g. winsorisation rescaling reference-scaled space) codebook_space argument identical somalign_query(). returned somalign_query object fully compatible somalign_fit(). sample_distance set NA (field read somalign_fit()). codebook non-linearly post-processed, reused assignments approximate: cells near node boundaries may flip, affects small fraction tail cells measurably impact OT alignment quality. somalign_sensitivity_grid() — direct projection cached across grid points. O(N × reference_nodes) projection query cells onto reference codebook identical every (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) combination, now computed grid loop reused. K-point grid saves (K − 1) projection passes. function signature otherwise unchanged (somalign_fit() parameters accepted explicitly; ... forwarding removed).

title: somalign 0.99.3
text: somalign_reference_from_som() — zero-reprojection reference constructor trained kohonen SOM objects. Reuses information already stored trained object: node masses som$unit.classif (exact counts every training cell computational cost), label probabilities supervised Y-layer codebook codes[[2]] (enables label transfer without passing labels vector), per-node distance thresholds recomputed reference-scaled X-space embedded som$data[[1]]. distance computation O(N × p) — single subtraction per cell already-known assigned node — argmax O(N × nodes) memory peak. 44.6 M-cell pilot cohort eliminates bottleneck previously required subsampling 100 k cells. existing somalign_reference() somalign_reference_from_nodes() APIs unchanged. Note: xyf/supersom SOM trained equal X+Y layer weights, unit.classif reflects joint supervised assignment. Node masses therefore match SOM’s partition rather pure-X nearest-node assignment. Distance quantiles computed X-space outside-reference thresholds remain scale somalign_fit()’s query distances.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: Default epsilon lowered 0.5 0.1 somalign_fit(), somalign_fit_anchored(), somalign_som_stability(). cost normalisation added v0.99.1, epsilon = 0.5 left transport plan diffuse: Nuñez 2023 full-spectrum flow CyTOF benchmarks, label posteriors collapsed onto reference class prior CyTOF acceptance dropped zero. epsilon = 0.1 minority classes transfer correctly corrected JS divergence 4× lower. Code sets epsilon explicitly unaffected; relied default, re-check acceptance rates correction norms. Default epsilon_global somalign_fit_two_pass() lowered 0.5 0.3. global pass aligns coarse structure tolerates smoothing local pass, keeps larger epsilon single-pass default.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() gains solver = "log_domain" option. log-domain Sinkhorn variant works log-potentials ff (query) gg (reference) rather primal scaling vectors, avoiding exp(-C/epsilon) kernel underflow entirely. slower per iteration tolerates cost/epsilon ratios cause default "internal" solver warn. somalign_som_stability() — new function sweeps vector random seeds query SOM training, holds reference fixed, returns per-seed summary data frame. Reports transport_mass, mean_match_fraction, max_row_mass_error, accepted_label_fraction, outside_direct_fraction, outside_corrected_fraction, mean_correction_norm, converged seed, quantifying run--run variance SOM training randomness — largest uncontrolled variance source somalign workflow.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() diagnostics now include two scale-invariant marginal-error fields: diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_row_error diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_col_error. normalise absolute marginal violations total query reference mass respectively, making easier distinguish expected mass destruction solver non-convergence.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: vignette("algorithm") gains new Limitations OT correction section covering barycentric contraction (correction target conditional mean contracts toward dense reference nodes grows epsilon), unpaired OT design (mass distributions, individual samples, matched — anchor-based methods exploit sample-level pairing), uncalibrated novelty score (match_fraction reflects epsilon/rho regime much true novel population).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() default epsilon changed 0.05 0.5. cost matrix now normalised median positive entry Sinkhorn kernel computed, making epsilon scale- dimension-invariant across feature sets different size spread. Users set epsilon explicitly relative raw codebook distances re-tune normalised scale (inspect diagnostics$solver$cost_scale).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_query() gains codebook_space argument (default "reference_scaled"). user-supplied som_query provided, setting codebook_space = "raw" instructs somalign_query() re-scale codebook reference-scaled space, mirroring existing behaviour somalign_reference(). somalign_fit() diagnostics now include diagnostics$solver$converged (logical), diagnostics$solver$final_delta (final iterate change), diagnostics$solver$cost_scale (cost normalisation factor).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() now emits warning 50 % query mass destroyed transport, 50 % query samples fall outside reference distance thresholds. non-finite Sinkhorn iterate delta now triggers explicit warning instead finishing silently (previously, NaN delta suppressed early-break condition non-convergence warning). somalign_reference_from_nodes() now emits informational messages label probabilities distance quantiles supplied, users aware label transfer outside-reference detection disabled. internal OT input validator now warns query reference node masses zero, preventing silent -zero transport plan. Fixed silent fallback .somalign_thresholds(): requested distance-quantile column absent, warning now emitted rather silently substituting third column position.

title: somalign 0.99.0
text: Added somalign_train_reference() somalign_reference() train wrap existing kohonen SOM fixed reference object, including node mass, per-node label probability, per-node distance quantile summaries. Added somalign_reference_from_nodes() reconstruct reference object pre-computed node-level artifacts (codebook, center, scale, masses, label probabilities, distance quantiles) without needing original data. Added somalign_query() scale query data reference parameters, optionally train query SOM, return somalign_query object containing per-sample node assignments distances. Added somalign_fit() align query SOM fixed reference SOM via codebook-level unbalanced entropic optimal transport. Uses internal pure-R generalized Sinkhorn solver; "auto" retained compatibility alias internal solver. Added somalign_results() extract per-sample data frame direct reference projection columns (canonical) transport-corrected projection columns (auxiliary, annotation visualisation). Added somalign_diagnostics() return solver, OT plan, node-level, projection diagnostics somalign_fit object. Added somalign_sensitivity_grid() sweep grid OT hyperparameters (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) return summary data frame tuning stability assessment. Supports optional parallelisation via BiocParallel::bplapply() parallel = TRUE.
